title: Stand up the clean-room fa player repo
summary: Created the replacement repo for the FrameAngel player lane and cut the first clean version line at 0.1.1.
reasoning: The player lane had accumulated too much monorepo drift, recovery baggage, and cross-lane contamination to keep stabilizing it in place. This version established a bounded replacement repo with only the compile surface, asset/export lane, release wrapper, and documentation needed to keep shipping the player again.

changes.summary: Imported the bounded player runtime, minimal asset lane, and shared release helpers into the new fa repo.
changes.reasoning: The goal was a replacement repo for the player, not another partial mirror of the monorepo.

changes.summary: Reset the clean-room repo version line to 0.1.1 and made versioned filenames the authority seam.
changes.reasoning: The old 0.0.x line belonged to the previous repo history. The replacement repo needed its own deterministic version line.

changes.summary: Established the minimal screen-core release wrapper and validation receipts for the versioned assetbundle plus matching plugin pair.
changes.reasoning: The new repo needed one bounded build/deploy path that could be trusted before any more feature recovery work.

verification: Built and deployed fa_cua_player.0.1.1.dll and fa_player_asset.0.1.1.assetbundle.
Recorded release manifest and validation receipts for 0.1.1 in the local build lane.
knownIssues: Aspect and presentation correctness still required follow-up live testing.
Transport parity and screen-core presentation still needed recovery against historical witnesses.
